context: LHT-SUBSTRATE-001 Phase 2: fresh Chutes primary deployment to replace the retired pre-genesis-d3-10 chute (KW-RELAY-CHUTES-D3-001 status mitigated-residual). Attempted under operator authorization 'Complete all' 2026-05-16. Pre-flight: Chutes credit balance $71.87 (target floor $5.00); 15/15 mocked test suites green; chutes CLI 0.6.8 bridged via WSL Ubuntu venv (~/.local/bin/chutes -> ~/chutes-venv/bin/chutes) because chutes/entrypoint/ssh.py imports the Unix-only pwd module at module-load time, breaking the CLI on Windows-native Python.

attempts.platform_error: Only TEE chutes are supported as of 2026-05-12, please set tee=True
attempts.warmup: timed out 600s polling retired d3-10 endpoint (returns 502 — chute deleted 2026-05-13)
attempts.remediation_landed: Added tee=True to Chute(...) in xion_relay_chute.py:155

attempts.platform_error: TEE private deployments are limited at this time due to infrastructure capacity limitations.
attempts.remediation_unresolved: Chutes platform capacity-limits TEE-private deployments. Workaround: deploy --public (anyone can invoke the chute). Posture change requires operator security review — XION_API_REQUIRE_BEARER=false on the chute means no app-level auth, only chute-platform auth gating.

wallet_state_final.note: Balance unchanged from pre-attempt $71.8729 — zero fee charged across both attempts (Chutes rejected both pre-billing).

findings: Chutes platform added a hard TEE requirement on 2026-05-12 (4 days before this attempt). Existing chute module needed tee=True kwarg.

Even with tee=True, Chutes platform is capacity-limited on TEE-PRIVATE deployments as of 2026-05-16. Public TEE deployments may still be accepted, but flipping to --public requires operator security review because the chute itself has XION_API_REQUIRE_BEARER=false.
No fee charged across both attempts. Chutes platform rejects pre-billing when the policy/capacity check fails.
chutes 0.6.8 CLI imports pwd at module-load (chutes/entrypoint/ssh.py:2) — breaks on Windows-native Python. WSL venv install (~/chutes-venv) + ~/.local/bin symlink unblocks xion_ops Win-WSL bridge.

The retired pre-genesis-d3-10 chute subdomain (nikhilkadalge-xion-relay-pre-genesis-d3.chutes.ai) still serves HTTP 502 from the Chutes edge — the URL is sticky even though the chute was deleted 2026-05-13. Warmup polling against the deleted endpoint timed out 600s both attempts.

Phase 2 deploy is PLATFORM-BLOCKED until either (a) operator authorizes --public deployment, OR (b) Chutes TEE-private capacity returns. Phase 3 + Phase 4 wiring proceeded on the assumption of an honest-failed first drill row.
decision_implications_for_LHT_SUBSTRATE_: Phase 4 first drill row will land with BOTH probes failed (Akash GPU floor unreachable per 2026-05-14 evidence; Chutes secondary returns 502 from dead endpoint). Substantive pass-evidence is verifier_results[substrate-portability]=exit 0 — the structural attestation that the harness works and the registry shape is valid. This matches the operator-confirmed honest-interim posture 2026-05-16 ('accept failed-honest row').
operational_artefacts_persisted: Phase 3 drill-script semantic swap landed (chutes=primary, akash=secondary in row labels; registry untouched per genesis ordering doctrine)
Phase 4 GH Actions workflow .github/workflows/immortality-drill.yml authored (per DD-004 (c))
Cloud-init template moved to archive/scripts/ with header note pointing at the workflow
next_recommended: Operator decision: authorize --public TEE deployment OR wait for TEE-private capacity to return. The chute is functionally public-protocol (Relay /health, /quote, /self are designed to be publicly probable); --public surfaces a posture difference but does NOT add a privacy regression vs the prior d3-10 deployment posture.

If --public authorized: re-run `python -m xion_ops deploy relay-chutes --accept-fee --public --no-publish-registry`. Capture chute_id/url/version, then publish to registry via the same deployer (drop --no-publish-registry) or via xion_ops.deployers.relay_chutes RelayRegistryPublisher.

If waiting: Phase 4 weekly cron will continue to produce honest-failed rows (verifiers green, both probes red). That's still 1-of-3 closure-grade attestation rows per closure plan 'Beyond Phase 5' item 1; the row attests harness mechanics from a non-operator-fingerprint.
Either way: flip KW-RELAY-CHUTES-D3-001 from mitigated-residual to either `mitigated` (if --public ships) or `open` (if platform capacity blocks indefinitely and this becomes a real weakness).
